title: alchemilla pubescens var. pusilla
intro: Alchemilla pubescens var. pusilla, also known as dwarf lady's mantle, is a low-growing perennial plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations, reducing swelling, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Alchemilla pubescens var. pusilla can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
